title: cynoglossum castaneum
intro: Cynoglossum castaneum, also known as Chestnut Tongue Root or Chestnut Hound's Tongue, is a perennial plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral activities. The plant's health benefits include soothing respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, while also supporting the immune system and reducing fever. In herbal preparations, Cynoglossum castaneum is often dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
